title: rubus pilosus var. schlechtendalii
intro: Rubus pilosus var. schlechtendalii, also known as a type of wild bramble or black raspberry, is a perennial shrub native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rubus pilosus var. schlechtendalii include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, as well as its potential to help manage conditions such as diarrhea and fever. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used in folk medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
